claim: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for BMI.

corpus.snippet: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for B...
corpus.content: High sugar intake was associated with a 22% increased diabetes risk after adjusting for BMI.

corpus.title: News: Global Nutrition Consortium — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22...
corpus.content:
Global Nutrition Consortium ANNOUNCES KEY SCIENTIFIC UPDATE

A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for BMI. The announcement comes as changes in the scientific sector. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.content:
Global Nutrition Consortium ANNOUNCES CONTINUED SCIENTIFIC CONTROVERSY

Multiple sources now confirm: A meta-analysis found that diets high in processed sugar increased the risk of Type 2 diabetes by 22%, controlling for BMI. The announcement comes as reports circulating about scientific. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.
